quick_skim.title: TACO: Towards Task-Consistent Open-Vocabulary Adaptation in Video Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Adapting CLIP for open-vocabulary video recognition necessitates a delicate balance between newly acquired video knowledge and the pretrained generalization. While existing studies pursue this generalization-specialization trade-off with additional regularizations or constraints, we argue that they overlook the deviation of representations beyond the fine-tuning data distribution, resulting in suboptimal adaptation effects. We believe such deviation is inherited from the inconsistency between the fine-tuning and evaluation objectives, where model optimization is restricted to the known training distribution but evaluated on unseen ones. In this paper, we introduce \emph{TACO}, a simple yet effective framework to mitigate the potential negative effects induced by this inconsistency. Our key insight is that adaptation should preserve OOD-relevant alignment beyond the training distribution. To this end, we propose \emph{Relative Structure Distillation}, which regularizes the relative geometry of the representation space and suppresses harmful alignment shift during training. We further decouple the representation space from the optimization space with a lightweight specialization projection, allowing task-specific adaptation without directly overspecializing the representations used at test time. \emph{TACO} establishes state-of-the-art performance on diverse benchmarks under cross-dataset and base-to-novel settings. Code will be released at https://github.com/ZMHH-H/TACO.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: open-vocabulary video recognition addressing adaptation to unseen classes, similar to zero-shot action recognition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 开放词汇视频识别，适应未见类别，类似零样本动作识别
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 开放词汇视频识别，适应未见类别，类似零样本动作识别
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 开放词汇视频识别，适应未见类别，类似零样本动作识别
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes TACO framework for open-vocabulary video recognition by aligning fine-tuning and evaluation objectives, enabling generalization to unseen classes; relevant as a zero-shot video understanding method.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对开放词汇视频识别中微调与评估目标不一致导致泛化差的问题，提出TACO框架。TACO通过缓解表示分布偏差，使模型在适应视频知识的同时保持预训练泛化能力。实验表明在多个视频识别基准上达到先进零样本/开放词汇性能，为视频理解中的零样本学习提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对开放词汇视频识别中微调与评估目标不一致导致泛化差的问题，提出TACO框架。TACO通过缓解表示分布偏差，使模型在适应视频知识的同时保持预训练泛化能力。实验表明在多个视频识别基准上达到先进零样本/开放词汇性能，为视频理解中的零样本学习提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 零样本组合动作识别, especially work that connects or combines: zero-shot learning for action recognition; compositional action recognition methods; zero-shot action recognition with composition; zero shot compositional action recognition methods; learning to recognize unseen action compositions; compositional action recognition without training samples.

quick_skim.title: T-MOR: Learning Motion-Aware Skeleton Representations for Human Action Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Vision-language models such as CLIP have recently achieved strong performance on a wide range of visual understanding tasks. However, most existing models rely primarily on appearance-level supervision from images or videos, and do not explicitly model human motion, which is essential for fine-grained and human-centric action recognition task as actions are defined by temporally structured and physically grounded body movements. To address this problem, we propose Transferable skeleton MOtion Representation (T-MOR), a motion-aware framework that learns transferable action representations from skeleton sequences with the aid of video and language supervision during training. T-MOR adopts a multi-modal contrastive learning scheme that aligns skeleton motion with visual and textual representations, while performing inference using only lightweight skeleton inputs. To support large-scale pre-training, we construct PoseCap-1M, a new dataset that contains over one million synchronized video, skeleton, and text triplets covering diverse human activities. We evaluate T-MOR on a range of human-centric action recognition benchmarks, including action classification and frame-wise temporal detection. Experimental results show that T-MOR consistently improves performance across multiple datasets, such as Toyota Smarthome, Penn Action, UAV-Human, TSU, and Charades. In addition, T-MOR demonstrates strong generalization ability in few-shot and zero-shot settings, highlighting the effectiveness of motion-centric and embodied representations for transferable action understanding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transferable skeleton representations for action recognition via multi-modal contrastive learning with language
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过多模态对比学习与语言对齐，学习可迁移的骨架动作表示
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过多模态对比学习与语言对齐，学习可迁移的骨架动作表示
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过多模态对比学习与语言对齐，学习可迁移的骨架动作表示
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: T-MOR learns motion-aware skeleton representations for human action recognition by aligning skeleton, video, and language via contrastive learning, enabling transferable action recognition.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前动作识别方法多依赖外观信息，忽略人体运动建模。本文提出T-MOR，一个运动感知框架，通过多模态对比学习对齐骨架序列、视频和语言，学习可迁移的动作表示。在多个动作识别基准上，该方法取得了优异性能，表明显式建模运动对提升动作识别泛化能力至关重要。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前动作识别方法多依赖外观信息，忽略人体运动建模。本文提出T-MOR，一个运动感知框架，通过多模态对比学习对齐骨架序列、视频和语言，学习可迁移的动作表示。在多个动作识别基准上，该方法取得了优异性能，表明显式建模运动对提升动作识别泛化能力至关重要。
